page.title: The Perfect 8-Hour Yacht Day in Marbella (Step-by-Step)

data.meta_description: Plan the perfect full day yacht itinerary Marbella with our step-by-step guide. From Puerto Banús to Cala Cortés, enjoy 8 hours of sun, sea, and luxury for €2,299.
data.summary: A detailed step-by-step guide to an 8-hour yacht charter in Marbella, covering the best anchorages, dining options, and onboard inclusions for a seamless day on the water.
key_facts: 8-hour charters from €2,299 (Tier A boats, all inclusive).
Departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella's luxury marina.
Typical route: Marbella coastline to Cala Cortés and back.
Skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance included.
Maximum 11 guests on Azimut 39, 9 on Astondoa 40.
Anchor stops at Cala del Faro, Cabopino, or Estepona.
Optional jet ski upgrade on Mangusta 80 (Tier B).
Book via WhatsApp for custom itineraries and availability.

faq.q: What is included in an 8-hour yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Just bring your swimwear and sunscreen.

faq.q: How much does a full day yacht itinerary in Marbella cost?
faq.a: For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), an 8-hour charter costs €2,299. Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 start at €4,719 for 4 hours. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Where does the yacht depart from?
faq.a: All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. You'll meet the skipper at the dock, usually 15 minutes before departure.
Yes, the skipper can adjust the route based on weather, sea conditions, and your preferences. Popular stops include Cala del Faro, Cabopino, and Estepona. Just let us know when booking.

faq.q: What is the maximum number of guests on a full day charter?
faq.a: Guest capacity varies by boat: Azimut 39 takes up to 11 guests, Astondoa 40 up to 9, and Mangusta 80 up to 12. Check individual boat pages for exact limits.

faq.q: Do we need to bring food or drinks?
faq.a: No, all charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring your own food or arrange catering through us.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on our charter day?
faq.a: Safety is our priority. If conditions are unsafe, we will reschedule or offer a full refund. The skipper will advise you before departure.

faq.q: How do I book a full day yacht itinerary in Marbella?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form on our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We will confirm availability and send payment details.

data.body_html:
An 8-hour yacht charter is the ultimate way to experience Marbella's coastline. With a full day on the water, you can swim in secluded coves, enjoy a leisurely lunch, and soak up the Mediterranean sun without rushing. This step-by-step guide shows you exactly how to plan your perfect full day yacht itinerary Marbella style, from departure at Puerto Banús to sunset return.

Why Choose an 8-Hour Charter?

Eight hours gives you time to explore beyond the immediate Marbella shoreline. You can cruise east to Cala Cortés or west to Estepona, with multiple anchor stops. Our Tier A boats, like the Astondoa 40 (€2,299 for 8 hours) or the Azimut 39, include everything you need: skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. No hidden costs.

Step 1: Morning Departure from Puerto Banús

Your day starts at Puerto Banús, Marbella's most glamorous marina. Arrive 15 minutes early to meet your skipper. You'll be welcomed aboard with a glass of cava and a safety briefing. By 10:00, you're cruising west along the Golden Mile, with views of La Concha mountain and the luxury villas of the Sierra Blanca foothills.

Step 2: First Swim Stop – Cala del Faro or Cabopino

After 30–40 minutes, you reach Cala del Faro, a rocky cove with crystal-clear water. Drop anchor and dive in. The water temperature in summer averages 24–26 °C. Alternatively, head to Cabopino's sandy beach, quieter than the central Marbella strips. Your skipper will recommend the best spot based on wind and swell.

Step 3: Mid-Morning Cruise to Cala Cortés

Continue east towards Cala Cortés, a stunning bay just past the Río Verde estuary. The cruise takes about 45 minutes at 20 knots. The coastline here is less developed, with pine trees down to the shore. Keep an eye out for dolphins – they're common in these waters.

Step 4: Lunch Anchorage – Estepona or Sotogrande Option

If you prefer a longer cruise, head west to Estepona (about 1 hour from Puerto Banús). Anchor off the old town's beach and enjoy a picnic lunch onboard. Your included snacks and drinks keep everyone happy, or you can bring your own food. For a more exclusive vibe, continue to Sotogrande, though that adds another 30 minutes each way.

Step 5: Afternoon Relaxation and Water Activities

After lunch, find a calm bay for swimming, paddleboarding, or just sunbathing on the foredeck. On a Tier B boat like the Mangusta 80 (from €4,719 for 4 hours), a jet ski is included free. On other boats, you can bring your own inflatables or snorkel gear. The skipper will position the boat for maximum comfort.

Step 6: Sunset Cruise Back to Puerto Banús

Around 17:00, start your return journey. The late afternoon light on the Marbella coast is spectacular – the mountains turn gold, and the sea calms. Arrive back at Puerto Banús around 18:00, just in time to see the marina come alive for the evening. Your skipper will dock and you can disembark at your leisure.

What to Bring for Your Full Day Yacht Itinerary Marbella

  • Swimwear and towel
  • Sunscreen (SPF 50+) and sunglasses
  • Hat and light cover-up
  • Camera or phone (waterproof case recommended)
  • Extra snacks if you have dietary preferences
  • Light jacket for the evening breeze

Booking Your 8-Hour Charter

Ready to plan your day? Browse our full fleet and choose the boat that fits your group. For Tier A prices are fixed at €2,299 for 8 hours. For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. We recommend booking at least a week in advance during July and August.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in an 8-hour yacht charter in Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Just bring your swimwear and sunscreen.

How much does a full day yacht itinerary in Marbella cost?

For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), an 8-hour charter costs €2,299. Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 start at €4,719 for 4 hours. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Where does the yacht depart from?

All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. You'll meet the skipper at the dock, usually 15 minutes before departure.

Can we customise the 8-hour itinerary?

Yes, the skipper can adjust the route based on weather, sea conditions, and your preferences. Popular stops include Cala del Faro, Cabopino, and Estepona. Just let us know when booking.

What is the maximum number of guests on a full day charter?

Guest capacity varies by boat: Azimut 39 takes up to 11 guests, Astondoa 40 up to 9, and Mangusta 80 up to 12. Check individual boat pages for exact limits.

Do we need to bring food or drinks?

No, all charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring your own food or arrange catering through us.

What happens if the weather is bad on our charter day?

Safety is our priority. If conditions are unsafe, we will reschedule or offer a full refund. The skipper will advise you before departure.

How do I book a full day yacht itinerary in Marbella?

Contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form on our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We will confirm availability and send payment details.
